entries.definition:
كتاب الميم

مسط - مسك

(مسك) الميم والسين والكاف أصلٌ واحد صحيح يدلُّ على حَبْس الشيء أو تحبُّسه. والبَخِيل مُمسِكٌ. والإمساك: البُخْل؛ وكذا المَسَاك والمِسَاك والمَسِيك : البخيلُ أيضاً ورجل مُسَكةٌ، إذ كان لا يَعلَق بشيءٍ فيتخلَّص منه. والمَسَك: السِّوار من الذَّبْل: لاستمساكِهِ باليدِ، الواحدةُ مَسَكة. قال:

appreciate (in an interpretation of verse 56:79) لَا يَمَسُّهُ إِلَّا الْمُطَهَّرُونَ
none but the purified can comprehend it.

يَتَمَاسَّ yatamāss [imperf. of v. VI, تَمَاسَّ tamāssā, intrans.] to have
intercourse with another person (58:3) فَتَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَتَمَاسَّا the
freeing of a slave before the two of them touch one another.

مَسّ mass [n./v. n.] touching, affliction (2:275) الَّذِي يَتَخَبَّطُهُ الشَّيْطَانُ
مِنَ الْمَسِّ the one whom Satan confounds by [his] touch.

مِسَاس misās [v. n.] touch, touching *(20:97) لَا مِسَاسَ
[interjection] ‘there shall be no touching!’, I shall not touch nor
will I be touched (an expression originally used in Arabia at times
of epidemics).
entries.definition_english:
appreciate (in an interpretation of verse 56:79) لَا يَمَسُّهُ إِلَّا الْمُطَهَّرُونَ
none but the purified can comprehend it.

يَتَمَاسَّ yatamāss [imperf. of v. VI, تَمَاسَّ tamāssā, intrans.] to have
intercourse with another person (58:3) فَتَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَتَمَاسَّا the
freeing of a slave before the two of them touch one another.

مَسّ mass [n./v. n.] touching, affliction (2:275) الَّذِي يَتَخَبَّطُهُ الشَّيْطَانُ
مِنَ الْمَسِّ the one whom Satan confounds by [his] touch.

مِسَاس misās [v. n.] touch, touching *(20:97) لَا مِسَاسَ
[interjection] ‘there shall be no touching!’, I shall not touch nor
will I be touched (an expression originally used in Arabia at times
of epidemics).

entries.definition:
m–s–k hide (originally that of young sheep); bracelet, anklet; a
catch, to hold, to seize, to firmly adhere to, to be guided by; to be
holding together; brain; to stop doing; to be miserly; deposit; to
be impregnable; water reservoir; musk; to scent. Although
classified under this root, مِسْك misk is recognised by many
philologists as an early borrowing from Persian. Of this root,
eight forms occur 27 times in the Qur’an: يُمَسِّكُونَ yumassikūn
once; أَمْسَكَ ʾamsaka 18 times; اسْتَمْسَكَ istamsaka three times;
إِمْسَاك ʾimsāk once; مُمْسِك mumsik once; مُمْسِكَات mumsikāt once;
مُسْتَمْسِكُونَ mustamsikūn once and مِسْك misk once.

يُمَسِّك yumassik [imperf. of v. II مَسَّكَ massaka, trans.] to hold
fast, to resolutely adhere to (7:170) وَالَّذِينَ يُمَسِّكُونَ بِالْكِتَابِ as for those
who hold fast to the Book.

أَمْسَكَ ʾamsaka [v. IV] I [intrans.] 1 to be niggardly, to be
miserly, to be tight-fisted (17:100) لَوْ أَنْتُمْ تَمْلِكُونَ خَزَائِنَ رَحْمَةِ رَبِّي إِذًا
لَأَمْسَكْتُمْ if you possessed the treasures of the mercy of my
Lord, you would become tight-fisted for fear of spending 2 to
cease, to withhold هَذَا عَطَاؤُنَا فَامْنُنْ أَوْ أَمْسِكْ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ this is our gift, do
what you wish with it [lit. bestow or withhold it] without reckoning
II [trans.] 1 to hold, to keep, to retain (33:37) أَمْسِكْ عَلَيْكَ زَوْجَكَ وَاتَّقِ
اللَّهَ hold fast to your wife and be mindful of God 2 to hold back, to
withhold, to stop (35:2) مَا يَفْتَحِ اللَّهُ لِلنَّاسِ مِنْ رَحْمَةٍ فَلَا مُمْسِكَ لَهَا وَمَا يُمْسِكْ

884
